title: cornus mas var. albocarpa
intro: Cornus mas var. albocarpa, also known as the White Cornel or Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Cornaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cornus mas var. albocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb for treating various conditions. In herbal preparations, Cornus mas var. albocarpa is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in combination with other herbs to create remedies for digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
